entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: Domain/company for which this career development principle applies

entities.name: Self-reflection
entities.context: Cognitive activity subject to high activation energy barrier; requires forcing function to initiate

entities.name: Activation energy barrier
entities.context: Psychological barrier preventing expensive tasks like self-reflection without immediate necessity

entities.name: Hyperbolic discounting
entities.context: Cognitive bias explaining why future utility of preparation is underweighted until deadline approaches

entities.name: Identity work
entities.context: Self-modeling and narrative extraction; expensive cognitive tasks avoided without forcing function

entities.name: Just-in-time interventions
entities.context: Product design approach attaching to high-stakes moments when psychological barriers are lowest

entities.name: Just-in-case platforms
entities.context: Product approach relying on abstract readiness desire; contrasted with just-in-time

entities.name: Forcing function
entities.context: High-stakes pressure or immediate necessity that overcomes reflection inertia

entities.name: Career tools
entities.context: Tools designed for career development that must solve for reflection inertia

relationships.context: Self-reflection is blocked by activation energy that utility alone cannot overcome
Hyperbolic discounting applied to identity work explains deferred self-reflection
Career tools should be designed as just-in-time interventions rather than just-in-case platforms
Just-in-time approach leverages forcing functions like interview scheduling
Forcing functions overcome activation energy barriers by creating immediate necessity
Core product strategy principle for Careerspan career tools
